methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nano One Materials is a public climate and energy company listed on the TSX (ticker: LNZA) that has raised approximately C$100M to date. The valuation analysis operates within a fragmented peer universe comprising one public comparable and eight private frontier-tech peers in adjacent energy and materials sectors, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

The peer-median approach is not applicable to this valuation. The climate and energy sector contains 21 public and private companies broadly, but rigorous screening for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category—below the minimum threshold for reliable median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given Nano One's specialized position in advanced materials for energy storage and battery manufacturing, which does not map cleanly to broader climate peers.

Without triangulation via peer multiples, valuation support depends on revenue or earnings multiples (if available in the data), discounted cash flow analysis, or precedent transaction pricing within the frontier-tech materials subsector. The analysis is constrained by the thinness of public trading comparables and the heterogeneity of private peers across different technology maturity stages. Key uncertainties center on commercialization timelines, capital intensity of scaling, and technology risk differentiation relative to the stated peer group.
